hypothesis: Incorporating dynamic, real-time spectroscopic monitoring (e.g., operando Raman or infrared spectroscopy) of the cathode-electrolyte interface into the closed-loop optimization framework will enable the identification of transient intermediate species and local pH gradients that correlate with reactor yield, thereby revealing hidden descriptor variables that can further improve optimization convergence and maximum achievable reactor yield beyond the current 44 mA cm⁻².
